misconceptionSummary: Students see each picture separately instead of seeing the structural growth from one term to the next.

visuals.ruleText: A diagram sequence grows by a repeating visual rule
visuals.subText: Look for what stays the same and what changes

steps.narration: Diagram sequences are built from a repeated pattern of growth.
It helps to notice which parts of the diagram stay the same and which parts are added each time.

lines.text: If 2 tiles are added each time, the totals increase by 2

steps.narration: The visual growth creates the number pattern.

lines.text: Next diagram = previous diagram + same growth

steps.narration: Predicting the next term means extending the same structure one more step.

visuals.expression: Diagram growth and number growth are linked
visuals.label: See the structure, not just the picture

steps.narration: The key idea is that each new diagram follows the same structural rule.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: focusing on how the picture looks overall rather than identifying the repeated growth structure.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Try to describe exactly what is added from one term to the next. That reveals the rule.
